asserts.log_grep_match: (none — binstall WARN: will be installed from source)

gate_disposition_note: Catalog row install/cargo-binstall has pre-P56 status=PARTIAL with blast_radius=P1 (works, just slow). Wave 3 measured the same status post-fix — neither regression nor full lift. Per the catalog's allowed-status set for this row, PARTIAL is acceptable; the validator's per-row gate (scripts/p56-validate-install-evidence.py) is configured to admit PARTIAL on this row only. The honest assertion booleans above (all `false`) reflect the actual rehearsal outcome and feed Wave 4's verifier subagent the un-massaged signal.

failure_mode.primary: binstall metadata in crates/reposix-cli/Cargo.toml and crates/reposix-remote/Cargo.toml is misaligned with the actual release.yml archive shape produced by Option A's version_tag rename. Specifically the [package.metadata.binstall] block has pkg-url = '{ repo }/releases/download/v{ version }/{ name }-{ target }.{ archive-format }' but release.yml uploads assets at 'releases/download/reposix-cli-v{ version }/reposix-v{ version }-{ target }.tar.gz'. Four mismatches:
failure_mode.mismatch_1_tag_prefix: template uses `v{ version }`, actual GH Release tag is `reposix-cli-v{ version }`
failure_mode.mismatch_2_archive_basename: template uses `{ name }` (resolves to `reposix-cli`), actual archive basename is `reposix-v{ version }` (no `-cli` suffix; the workspace produces a unified archive containing both reposix and git-remote-reposix binaries)
failure_mode.mismatch_3_target_glibc_vs_musl: binstall's default linux target is `x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`, but release.yml only ships `x86_64-unknown-linux-musl` archives — needs an explicit target override or musl-only metadata
failure_mode.mismatch_4_archive_extension: template emits `.tgz`, release.yml uploads `.tar.gz` (binstall handles tgz=tar.gz aliasing fine, but worth noting for future-Rust-version archive validators)
failure_mode.secondary: binstall's source-build fallback (`cargo install reposix-cli --version 0.11.3`) also failed because transitive dep `block-buffer 0.12.0` requires Cargo feature `edition2024` which is unstable in Rust 1.82 (the project's MSRV). This is an orthogonal MSRV-breakage bug, NOT in P56 scope — flagged for v0.12.1 carry-forward (MIGRATE-03 supplement: refresh transitive deps OR raise MSRV to 1.85).

binstall_install_log_excerpt: WARN The package reposix-cli v0.11.3 will be installed from source (with cargo)
WARN The package reposix-remote v0.11.3 will be installed from source (with cargo)

recovery.fix_outline: Update [package.metadata.binstall] in crates/reposix-cli/Cargo.toml and crates/reposix-remote/Cargo.toml: change pkg-url template to use the actual release.yml archive shape, and add explicit target overrides for x86_64-unknown-linux-musl + aarch64-unknown-linux-musl (since release.yml ships only musl Linux archives). Re-test against Rust 1.85+ container after MSRV bump or after refreshing transitive deps to cap block-buffer at <0.12.
